rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (3/10) and rest on the same substantive concerns: the unmet C1 German requirement, lack of executive-level leadership and formal team management, and missing enterprise IT security/compliance/UML experience. The employer-name swap (re:cinq→Naver, Optilyz→Tencent, GISCollective→MercadoLibre) produced no new concern, no score shift, and no reasoning keyed to the company names — the wording differences are ordinary run-to-run paraphrase.
